actionUnits.condition: Auto-ignite via neighbor influence. GameObject has BoxCollider (isTrigger=0), no Rigidbody, PrefabInstance of 'Fire-Wrapper'. Attached MonoBehaviour matches Fire.cs (m_Script fileID=11500000, guid=1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d). Serialized fields for this instance: debug=0, isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, timeToOn=3 (will be overwritten in Awake to Random[40,60]), downMultiplier=2, maxHitInterval=2, totalHitTime=5, onNeighborsThreshold=2, minNeighborDownDistance=3, mainEffectGo fileID=1849464941111748095. No UnityEvent m_Calls present on the component. To cover the turn-on path, at least 2 neighbor Fire components must be ON within maxNeighborDistance (instance-level override observed in PrefabInstance modifications suggests 3.5; component block shows 4), causing CheckNeighborState() -> shouldTurnOn=true and Update() to flip isOn after timeToOn. This action represents waiting for that automatic trigger; no direct public no-arg methods are available to manually invoke.

Extinguish via water hits. Fire implements IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3 normal) which requires repeated hits with intervals < maxHitInterval (2s) until currentHitTime >= totalHitTime (5s) to switch isOn=false and call UpdateFireState(true) -> ParticleSystem stop on mainEffectGo and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1). However, Fire exposes no public no-arg methods to call via Trigger. Requires an external water system (e.g., hose/particle) that invokes WaterHit on collisions. No UnityEvent m_Calls available on this component to drive this directly.
